channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Amazon just overtook Walmart in revenue for the first time
item.description: Amazon overtakes Walmart in terms of quarterly revenue, but how long will it last before it regains pole position?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Majority of Americans dislike Musk, Zuckerberg: Poll
item.description: A Pew Research Center poll shows 54% of Americans have an "unfavorable" view of Musk, while 67% have an "unfavorable" view of Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Leaked Nothing Phone 3a and 3a Pro renders tease a mid-range phone that should have Samsung worried
item.description: Thanks to a leaked render, we could have our first proper look at the Nothing Phone 3a and 3a Pro – and even by looks alone, they seem to be worthy challengers for Samsung’s mid-range Galaxy handsets.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Tesla recalling more than 375,000 vehicles due to power steering issue
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said that the recall is for certain 2023 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: US SEC Launches New Cyber and Emerging Technologies Unit
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission has formed a new unit to protect retail investors from cybercrime amid evolving threats in the tech landscape.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia's RTX 5000 GPUs continue to face severe supply issues, with RTX 5070 Ti reportedly being even worse for launch stock than the RTX 5080
item.description: Nvidia's RTX 5000 GPU launch is getting worse every day, it seems, as new reports indicate the RTX 5070 Ti is facing dire supply shortages.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Birmingham’s $48M Oracle ERP project turned into an epic failure
item.description: Birmingham City Council’s (BCC) troubled enterprise resource planning (ERP) system, built on Oracle software, has become a case study of how large-scale IT projects can go awry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The iPhone 17 could launch with a display upgrade that makes the iPhone 17 Pro seem questionable
item.description: A new rumor suggests the entire iPhone 17 family could launch with 120Hz displays, finally bringing Apple up to par with the competition years after high-refresh rate screens became standard for smartphones.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Bosch impulsa ‘6G Smart’ para mejorar la inspección visual de sus líneas de producción
item.description: El sector industrial continúa desplegando casos de uso donde la tecnología sirve de palanca para mejorar procesos internos y robustecer la experiencia del empleado. En este sentido, Bosch, junto al centro de investigación i2CAT, ha puesto en marcha el proyecto 6G Smart.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Rise of Open-Source AI Models: Perplexity's R1 1776 Challenges Censorship
item.description: Perplexity releases model R1 1776, an open-source version of Deepseek R1 with post-training to remove China's censorship and provide unbiased answers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Charred EV batteries from California wildfires spark concern about toxic waste contaminating drinking water
item.description: A water agency in charge of ground water for 2 million L.A. County residents has several concerns about hazardous waste processing from the Eaton fire.
item.dc:creator: Steve Scauzillo, The Mercury News

item.title: Playing by the rules of AI
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Juan Orlandini, Fast Company

item.title: Aryaka adds AI-powered observability to SASE platform
item.description: SASE vendor Aryaka is looking to alleviate network alert fatigue with a little help from AI. Today Aryaka announced its Winter 2025 platform update, introducing its new AI Observe tool for advanced threat detection and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Humanoid robots bust dance moves alongside humans
item.description: Unitree H1 robots dance at gala, blending AI and tradition. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the convergence of cutting-edge technology and traditional culture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia Tech researchers raise red flags about mixed-reality security
item.description: In a new study, researchers at Virginia Tech spelled out a variety of security hazards that could compromise mixed-reality systems. The researchers highlighted security threats involving the manipulation of virtual objects when users collaborated via mixed reality headsets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Want to transform networking? Empower the missing users
item.description: As 2025 kicked off, I wrote a column about the network vendor landscape – specifically, which networking players will step up and put their efforts into finding new applications with solid business benefits that could enable a network transformation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data center spending to top $1 trillion by 2029 as AI transforms infrastructure
item.description: Massive global demand for AI technology is causing data centers to increase spending on servers, power, and cooling infrastructure. As a result, data center CapEx spending will hit $1.1 trillion by 2029, up from $430 billion in 2024, according to Dell’Oro Group. Much of this growth is due to AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3 reasons to consider a data security posture management platform
item.description: A week rarely goes by without a major data security breach. Recent news includes a breach impacting an energy company’s 8 million customers, another compromising the information on 450,000 current and former students, and one more exposing 240,000 credit union members.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 key features of Postman’s AI Agent Builder
item.description: The software landscape is shifting from passive business processes to dynamic, AI-driven workflows. AI agents—systems that interact with APIs, make decisions, and execute complex tasks—are at the forefront of this transformation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How cyberscams are draining Americans wallets by the billions
item.description: Scams cost Americans an estimated $159 billion every year and average about $3,500 in losses per victim. The CyberGuy has advice to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
